name: MRA Grant Application

sections.instructions: Provide a concise, non-technical summary of the proposed research for a general audience.
sections.name: general_audience_abstract
sections.title: General Audience Abstract
search_queries: melanoma research summary for general audience
non-technical overview of melanoma prevention research
impact of melanoma early detection research
simplified explanation of melanoma diagnosis research

sections.instructions: Provide a technical abstract of the proposed research, including specific aims and expected outcomes. This abstract will be used by the MRA to provide a summary to the review committee. Maximum 2,000 characters.
sections.name: technical_abstract
sections.title: Technical Abstract
search_queries: melanoma research technical abstract aims outcomes
research aims and expected outcomes in melanoma
clinical translational research technical abstract melanoma

sections.instructions: Provide a list of keywords that describe your research. These will be used to index your proposal.
sections.name: keywords
sections.title: Keywords
search_queries: mechanisms of drug resistance in melanoma
biomarkers for melanoma diagnosis and prognosis

sections.instructions: List all key personnel involved in the project, including their roles and contact information. For PIs, Young Investigators, and Mentors ORCID IDs are required. Include everyone except the applicant who will contribute to the scientific development or execution of the project in a substantive, measurable way whether they receive salaries or compensation under the grant.
sections.name: key_personnel
sections.title: Key Personnel

sections.instructions: Provide the background and specific aims of your research project, including the scientific problem you're addressing and the specific goals of your study. This section should clearly articulate the research question and the overall objective of the proposed project.
sections.name: background_and_specific_aims
sections.title: Background and Specific Aims

sections.instructions: Describe the rationale for your proposed research and its potential impact on melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, and/or treatment. Explain how your research aligns with MRA's goals for near-term advancements. Highlight the potential clinical impact of your research.
sections.name: rationale_and_impact
sections.title: Rationale and Impact
search_queries: rationale for melanoma research and its potential impact
clinical impact of melanoma research advancements
translational research in melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, and treatment
melanoma research alignment with MRA goals
potential clinical significance of melanoma research findings
research impact on melanoma treatment outcomes
novel strategies for melanoma detection and diagnosis

sections.instructions: Outline the proposed timeline for your research project, including key milestones and deliverables. Include a brief protocol synopsis and timeline with milestones for clinical studies, if applicable. Note that IRB approval is not required at the time of application but is required before initial payments are made.
sections.name: timeline_and_milestones
sections.title: Timeline and Milestones
search_queries: melanoma research project timeline and milestones
timeline for clinical studies in melanoma
IRB approval timeline for melanoma research
research plan and timeline for melanoma

sections.instructions: Provide a summary of your project's budget. Include a brief justification of the major budget components and align it to the described research activities. For Academic-Industry Partnership applications, include the budget contributions from both MRA and the industry partner. Awards will not support indirect costs, overhead costs, or other similar institutional charges. Fringe benefits for personnel salaries are allowable. Also include any costs associated with compliance with MRA's data sharing policy.
sections.name: budget_summary
sections.title: Budget Summary
search_queries: personnel salary and fringe benefits for cancer research
data sharing compliance costs in grant proposals
budget justification for melanoma research grant
research proposal budget components and justification

sections.instructions: List all current and pending support for the Applicant. For Team Science applications, also include current and pending support for all Key Personnel (Principal Investigators and Young Investigators). Describe any overlap with the MRA proposal. For Young Investigator and Pilot applications, current and pending support is only required for the Applicant.
sections.name: current_and_pending_support
sections.title: Current and Pending Support
search_queries: current research grants and funding support

sections.instructions: Provide a statement (up to 2000 characters) of how the proposed research project fits within MRA's overall research portfolio.
sections.name: mra_portfolio_fit
sections.title: MRA Portfolio Fit
search_queries: fit of proposed melanoma research with MRA portfolio
alignment of project with MRA research priorities
strategic relevance of this study to MRA's mission
how this research complements existing MRA funded projects
translational impact within the MRA research framework
synergies with MRA's strategic research goals
contribution of this research to the MRA strategic plan
MRA portfolio alignment and potential for melanoma research impact
